{{notability|date=March 2023}}
{{Short description|Shinto sect}} {{nihongo|'''Misogikyo'''|禊教}} is a [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen Shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref>
It is considered a "purification sect" alongside {{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|Shinshu-kyo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>
Its name literally means [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] religion
The group is quite obscure today<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Sawada |first=Janine Anderson |date=1998 |title=Mind and Morality in Nineteenth-Century Japanese Religions: Misogi-Kyō and Maruyama-Kyō |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927 |journal=Philosophy East and West |volume=48 |issue=1 |pages=108–141 |doi=10.2307/1399927 |issn=0031-8221}}</ref> It is very ritual‑focused with little theoretical theology. In this way it contrasts with [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shinto]].<ref name=":0" /> It emphasizes the right state of mind and self‑control<ref name=":0" /> It has influence from {{ill|Confucian Shinto|ja|儒教神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucian Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but is its own tradition.<ref name=":0" />
== Sources == * https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927
== Overview == {{infobox organization | name = '''Misogikyo''' (禊教) | image = File:P1000772 misogi 2.JPG | image_size = 280 | image_caption = {{ill|Misogi Shrine|ja|身曾岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi Shrine|1=Misogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637232}} | image2 = | image_size2 = | image_caption2 = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E}}}} | abbreviation = | established = 1840 (Tenpō 11) | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} | status = | purpose = | headquarters = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]]小淵沢町上笹尾3401 | coords = {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage DMS|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E|display=inline,title}} | location = | language = | founder = {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} | notable_people = | affiliated_organizations = | institutions = | leader_title = Head Priest | leader_name = {{ill|Yasugi Sakata|ja|坂田安儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi Sakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | website = }}
'''Misogikyo''' (禊教) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] that inherits the teachings of {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, who studied Zen, physiognomy, medicine, and {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}}. It is under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology]] and is a {{ill|Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja|包括宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗教法人|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}}.
== Founder == {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} (Inoue Masakane, Founder). He was born on August 4, [[:en:1790|1790]] (Kansei 2) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]], {{ill|Nihonbashi Hamacho|ja|日本橋浜町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Hamacho|1=Nihonbashi-Hamachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508340}}, as the son of [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]] accountant and Ando Shintei (安東真鐵). He was adopted by a relative on his mother's side and took the Inoue surname. His father, Ando Shintei (父安藤真鐵), was a disciple of [[:en:Kamo no Mabuchi|Kamo Mafuchi]] and had studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]], {{ill|Medicine|ja|医術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medicine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 医療|1=health care|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31207}}, [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]], and [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]]. Although he also mastered the esoteric secrets of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], being already of advanced age, he was unable to pass them on and is said to have bequeathed to Masakane Inoue the task of mastering and spreading Shinto to the world.
== History == === Founder’s Training Period === During his youth, Masakane Inoue studied [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] and medicine under {{ill|Isono Kōdō|ja|磯野弘道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Kōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:1809|1809]] (Bunka 6), and from [[:en:1814|1814]] (Bunka 11) he learned physiognomy and methods of breath regulation from physiognomist {{ill|Mizuno Nanboku|ja|水野南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Nanboku|1=Nanboku Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549357}}. It is said that he also acquired agricultural techniques ([[:en:Agriculture|Agriculture]]) while traveling through various provinces. In [[:en:1833|1833]] (Tenpō 4), he heard Shinto teachings from an elderly woman residing in [[:en:Shitaya|Shimotani]] and {{ill|Ikenohata|ja|池之端|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenohata|1=Ikenohata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551510}}, and, following his father's will, resolved to master Shinto. In the following year, [[:en:1834|1834]] (Tenpō 5), he entered the {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}} and studied its teachings.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> In [[:en:1836|1836]] (Tenpō 7), he obtained a permit for Shinto worship (神拝式許状) from the Hakukawa family.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> In [[:en:1838|1838]] (Tenpō 9), he received a priestly permit (神職許状) and the complete esoteric transmission of the three types of ritual purification (天津祝詞太祝詞三種祓等の奥旨皆伝) from the Hakukawa family. He had many disciples; among them was [[:en:Matsudaira Munehide|Munehide Matsudaira]], who later became the Grand Priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] and was a disciple from the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] of the [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]].
=== Preaching by the Founder === On April 15, [[:en:1840|1840]] (Tenpō 11), Inoue became a Shinto priest at {{ill|Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|ja|梅田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|1=Umedashinmeigu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538638}} in [[:en:武州|Bushū]], {{ill|Adachi District|ja|足立郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi District|1=Adachi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637033}}, and began his preaching, which Misogikyo regards as the establishment of its faith. However, in November [[:en:1841|1841]] (Tenpō 12),<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> he, along with his wife {{ill|Otoko Anzai|ja|安西男也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko Anzai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his disciple the Miura couple, {{ill|Hayato Miura|ja|三浦隼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chizen Miura|ja|智善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizen Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was imprisoned by the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Temples and Shrines Magistrate]].<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> Although Hayato Miura died in prison, in February [[:en:1842|1842]] (Tenpō 13) Inoue and his companions were released. He was ordered to document the teachings he had imparted and submit them to the magistrate. Inoue authored the "Shinto: The Only Q&A" (two volumes, which later became the first canonical text of Misogikyo) and submitted it; however, the suspicions were not dispelled, and in November of the same year he was imprisoned again. In June [[:en:1843|1843]] (Tenpō 14), he was exiled to [[:en:Miyake-jima|Miyakejima]] as a penal exile ([[:en:Penal transportation|Exile]]). Disciples including Munehide Matsudaira sent supplies to support him, and Inoue sent letters from the island—which were later compiled as the "Legacy Collection" in seven volumes, becoming Misogikyo's second canonical text—to instruct his followers. He continued his preaching and medical activities on Miyakejima, but died there in [[:en:1849|1849]] (Kaei 2).<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref>
== Deities == === Religious Corporation Misogikyo & Misogi Shrine === * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōkami|ja|天照太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} (Amaterasu Ōkami) * Tennotoku Chitoku Nari‑mi‑sosogi Jizai‑shin (天徳地徳乍身曽岐自在神), the deity enshrining the founder, {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}

Deities|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}}) *** [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (Ame‑no‑Minakanushi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} (Takamimusubi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} (Kamimusubi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami) *** {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾神|lt=Izanagi|lt_ja=伊那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Haraito Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraito Ōkami|1=Haraedo no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588504}} (Haraito‑no‑Ōkami) *** [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusuna Ōkami]] (Ubusuna‑no‑Ōkami) * Founder Shrine ({{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|井上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22117841}}) ** Founder Inoue Masakane God *** Founder {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, the Great Divine Spirit ** Ancestral Divine Spirit of the Founder *** Ancestral and Generational Divine Spirits of the Founder **** Umasu Michitama‑no‑mikoto, Ando Shintei, venerable (Inoue Masakane's father) **** Chiyodamahime‑no‑mikoto, Ide Chiyotōjiko (Inoue Masakane's mother) **** Misosogi Tsu‑hime‑no‑mikoto, Anzai Otone (Inoue Masakane's wife) ** Ancestral Head Priest Divine Spirits of the Family *** Shōdō Iwachakukone‑no‑mikoto, Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, venerable (father of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Kareido Kōseki Dainin, Founder, Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, venerable (a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Kinshidō Kōji Konome‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's first head priest, Sakata Yasuji, venerable (son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) *** Tsugihokukyūnouchi‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's second head priest, Inui Kyūsaburō, venerable *** Tsugihoshinsakihiko‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's third head priest, Shiba Mashinju, venerable *** Mizumotokohikodomori‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's fourth head priest, Sakata Minoru, venerable (son of Sakata Yasuji) ** Generational Divine Spirits of the Misogikyo Administrators *** Tokoyo Kagami Hiko‑no‑mikoto, Miura Hayato, venerable (a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Tokoyo Kagami Hime‑no‑mikoto, Miura Ayanadōjiko (wife of Miura Hayato, disciple of Inoue Masakane) * Ancestral Spirit Hall (Clan Ancestor Spirits) ** Nationwide Clan Ancestral Divine Spirits
== Successive Religious Leaders == * First Religious Leader: Sakata Tetsu‑yasu (1872 (Meiji 5), established the Weishin Church Misogikyo and served as president. Founder) * Second Religious Leader: Sakata Yasuji (1894 (Meiji 27), first head priest. Son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) * Third Religious Leader: Inui Kyūsaburō (1900 (Meiji 33), second head priest) * Fourth Religious Leader: Shiba Mashinju (1916 (Taishō 5), third head priest) * Fifth Religious Leader: Sakata Minoru (1924 (Taishō 13), fourth head priest. Son of Sakata Yasuji) * Sixth Religious Leader: Sakata Yasugi (1957 (Shōwa 32), fifth head priest. Son of Sakata Minoru) * (Seventh Religious Leader): Sakata Yasuhiro (1988 (Shōwa 63), declared the succession of the lineage. Son of Sakata Yasugi)
== Religious Influence == According to the Ministry of Culture's "Religious Yearbook" for the first year of Reiwa, the adherents of Religious Corporation Misogikyo number 90,711, and those of Religious Corporation Shinto Misogikyo number 22,360. Combined, they amount to roughly one‑third of the prewar figures. In addition, due to the internal turmoil within Misogikyo mentioned above, several independent churches that split from the sect exist.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Misogikyo]][[en:Misogikyo]][[ja:禊教]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11591200]] Wikidata name: Misogikyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on [[:en:English Wikipedia|English Wikipedia]] {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}}. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Misogikyo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} here [[:every:Misogikyo|Misogikyo]]. {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー会話:Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User talk:Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} as a Wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} page may have been under a different title {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー会話:Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User talk:Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC submission|t||ts=20230320104937|u=Immanuelle|ns=118|demo=}}
{{notability|date=March 2023}}
{{Short description|Shinto sect}} {{nihongo|'''Misogikyo'''|禊教}} is a [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen Shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref>
It is considered a "purification sect" alongside {{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|Shinshu-kyo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>
Its name literally means [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] religion
The group is quite obscure today<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Sawada |first=Janine Anderson |date=1998 |title=Mind and Morality in Nineteenth-Century Japanese Religions: Misogi-Kyō and Maruyama-Kyō |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927 |journal=Philosophy East and West |volume=48 |issue=1 |pages=108–141 |doi=10.2307/1399927 |issn=0031-8221}}</ref> It is very ritual‑focused with little theoretical theology. In this way it contrasts with [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shinto]].<ref name=":0" /> It emphasizes the right state of mind and self‑control<ref name=":0" /> It has influence from {{ill|Confucian Shinto|ja|儒教神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucian Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but is its own tradition.<ref name=":0" />
== Sources == * https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927
== Overview == {{infobox organization | name = '''Misogikyo''' (禊教) | image = File:P1000772 misogi 2.JPG | image_size = 280 | image_caption = {{ill|Misogi Shrine|ja|身曾岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi Shrine|1=Misogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637232}} | image2 = | image_size2 = | image_caption2 = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E}}}} | abbreviation = | established = 1840 (Tenpō 11) | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} | status = | purpose = | headquarters = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]]小淵沢町上笹尾3401 | coords = {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage DMS|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E|display=inline,title}} | location = | language = | founder = {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} | notable_people = | affiliated_organizations = | institutions = | leader_title = Head Priest | leader_name = {{ill|Yasugi Sakata|ja|坂田安儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi Sakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | website = }}
'''Misogikyo''' (禊教) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] that inherits the teachings of {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, who studied Zen, physiognomy, medicine, and {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}}. It is under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology]] and is a {{ill|Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja|包括宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗教法人|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}}.
== Founder == {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} (Inoue Masakane, Founder). He was born on August 4, [[:en:1790|1790]] (Kansei 2) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]], {{ill|Nihonbashi Hamacho|ja|日本橋浜町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Hamacho|1=Nihonbashi-Hamachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508340}}, as the son of [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]] accountant and Ando Shintei (安東真鐵). He was adopted by a relative on his mother's side and took the Inoue surname. His father, Ando Shintei (父安藤真鐵), was a disciple of [[:en:Kamo no Mabuchi|Kamo Mafuchi]] and had studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]], {{ill|Medicine|ja|医術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medicine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 医療|1=health care|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31207}}, [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]], and [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]]. Although he also mastered the esoteric secrets of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], being already of advanced age, he was unable to pass them on and is said to have bequeathed to Masakane Inoue the task of mastering and spreading Shinto to the world.
== History == === Founder’s Training Period === During his youth, Masakane Inoue studied [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] and medicine under {{ill|Isono Kōdō|ja|磯野弘道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Kōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:1809|1809]] (Bunka 6), and from [[:en:1814|1814]] (Bunka 11) he learned physiognomy and methods of breath regulation from physiognomist {{ill|Mizuno Nanboku|ja|水野南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Nanboku|1=Nanboku Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549357}}. It is said that he also acquired agricultural techniques ([[:en:Agriculture|Agriculture]]) while traveling through various provinces. In [[:en:1833|1833]] (Tenpō 4), he heard Shinto teachings from an elderly woman residing in [[:en:Shitaya|Shimotani]] and {{ill|Ikenohata|ja|池之端|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenohata|1=Ikenohata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551510}}, and, following his father's will, resolved to master Shinto. In the following year, [[:en:1834|1834]] (Tenpō 5), he entered the {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}} and studied its teachings.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> In [[:en:1836|1836]] (Tenpō 7), he obtained a permit for Shinto worship (神拝式許状) from the Hakukawa family.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> In [[:en:1838|1838]] (Tenpō 9), he received a priestly permit (神職許状) and the complete esoteric transmission of the three types of ritual purification (天津祝詞太祝詞三種祓等の奥旨皆伝) from the Hakukawa family. He had many disciples; among them was [[:en:Matsudaira Munehide|Munehide Matsudaira]], who later became the Grand Priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] and was a disciple from the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] of the [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]].
=== Preaching by the Founder === On April 15, [[:en:1840|1840]] (Tenpō 11), Inoue became a Shinto priest at {{ill|Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|ja|梅田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|1=Umedashinmeigu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538638}} in [[:en:武州|Bushū]], {{ill|Adachi District|ja|足立郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi District|1=Adachi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637033}}, and began his preaching, which Misogikyo regards as the establishment of its faith. However, in November [[:en:1841|1841]] (Tenpō 12),<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> he, along with his wife {{ill|Otoko Anzai|ja|安西男也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko Anzai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his disciple the Miura couple, {{ill|Hayato Miura|ja|三浦隼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chizen Miura|ja|智善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizen Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was imprisoned by the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Temples and Shrines Magistrate]].<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> Although Hayato Miura died in prison, in February [[:en:1842|1842]] (Tenpō 13) Inoue and his companions were released. He was ordered to document the teachings he had imparted and submit them to the magistrate. Inoue authored the "Shinto: The Only Q&A" (two volumes, which later became the first canonical text of Misogikyo) and submitted it; however, the suspicions were not dispelled, and in November of the same year he was imprisoned again. In June [[:en:1843|1843]] (Tenpō 14), he was exiled to [[:en:Miyake-jima|Miyakejima]] as a penal exile ([[:en:Penal transportation|Exile]]). Disciples including Munehide Matsudaira sent supplies to support him, and Inoue sent letters from the island—which were later compiled as the "Legacy Collection" in seven volumes, becoming Misogikyo's second canonical text—to instruct his followers. He continued his preaching and medical activities on Miyakejima, but died there in [[:en:1849|1849]] (Kaei 2).<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref>

== Successive Religious Leaders == * First Religious Leader: Sakata Tetsu‑yasu (1872 (Meiji 5), established the Weishin Church Misogikyo and served as president. Founder) * Second Religious Leader: Sakata Yasuji (1894 (Meiji 27), first head priest. Son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) * Third Religious Leader: Inui Kyūsaburō (1900 (Meiji 33), second head priest) * Fourth Religious Leader: Shiba Mashinju (1916 (Taishō 5), third head priest) * Fifth Religious Leader: Sakata Minoru (1924 (Taishō 13), fourth head priest. Son of Sakata Yasuji) * Sixth Religious Leader: Sakata Yasugi (1957 (Shōwa 32), fifth head priest. Son of Sakata Minoru) * (Seventh Religious Leader): Sakata Yasuhiro (1988 (Shōwa 63), declared the succession of the lineage. Son of Sakata Yasugi)
== Religious Influence == According to the Ministry of Culture's "Religious Yearbook" for the first year of Reiwa, the adherents of Religious Corporation Misogikyo number 90,711, and those of Religious Corporation Shinto Misogikyo number 22,360. Combined, they amount to roughly one‑third of the prewar figures. In addition, due to the internal turmoil within Misogikyo mentioned above, several independent churches that split from the sect exist.

{{Short description|Shinto sect}} {{nihongo|'''Misogikyo'''|禊教}} is a [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen Shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref>
It is considered a "purification sect" alongside {{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|Shinshu-kyo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>
Its name literally means [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] religion
The group is quite obscure today<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Sawada |first=Janine Anderson |date=1998 |title=Mind and Morality in Nineteenth-Century Japanese Religions: Misogi-Kyō and Maruyama-Kyō |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927 |journal=Philosophy East and West |volume=48 |issue=1 |pages=108–141 |doi=10.2307/1399927 |issn=0031-8221}}</ref> It is very ritual‑focused with little theoretical theology. In this way it contrasts with [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shinto]].<ref name=":0" /> It emphasizes the right state of mind and self‑control<ref name=":0" /> It has influence from {{ill|Confucian Shinto|ja|儒教神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucian Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but is its own tradition.<ref name=":0" />
== Sources == * https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927
== Overview == {{infobox organization | name = '''Misogikyo''' (禊教) | image = File:P1000772 misogi 2.JPG | image_size = 280 | image_caption = {{ill|Misogi Shrine|ja|身曾岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi Shrine|1=Misogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637232}} | image2 = | image_size2 = | image_caption2 = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E}}}} | abbreviation = | established = 1840 (Tenpō 11) | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} | status = | purpose = | headquarters = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]]小淵沢町上笹尾3401 | coords = {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage DMS|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E|display=inline,title}} | location = | language = | founder = {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} | notable_people = | affiliated_organizations = | institutions = | leader_title = Head Priest | leader_name = {{ill|Yasugi Sakata|ja|坂田安儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi Sakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | website = }}
'''Misogikyo''' (禊教) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] that inherits the teachings of {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, who studied Zen, physiognomy, medicine, and {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}}. It is under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology]] and is a {{ill|Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja|包括宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗教法人|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}}.
== Founder == {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} (Inoue Masakane, Founder). He was born on August 4, [[:en:1790|1790]] (Kansei 2) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]], {{ill|Nihonbashi Hamacho|ja|日本橋浜町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Hamacho|1=Nihonbashi-Hamachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508340}}, as the son of [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]] accountant and Ando Shintei (安東真鐵). He was adopted by a relative on his mother's side and took the Inoue surname. His father, Ando Shintei (父安藤真鐵), was a disciple of [[:en:Kamo no Mabuchi|Kamo Mafuchi]] and had studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]], {{ill|Medicine|ja|医術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medicine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 医療|1=health care|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31207}}, [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]], and [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]]. Although he also mastered the esoteric secrets of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], being already of advanced age, he was unable to pass them on and is said to have bequeathed to Masakane Inoue the task of mastering and spreading Shinto to the world.
== History == === Founder’s Training Period === During his youth, Masakane Inoue studied [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] and medicine under {{ill|Isono Kōdō|ja|磯野弘道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Kōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:1809|1809]] (Bunka 6), and from [[:en:1814|1814]] (Bunka 11) he learned physiognomy and methods of breath regulation from physiognomist {{ill|Mizuno Nanboku|ja|水野南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Nanboku|1=Nanboku Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549357}}. It is said that he also acquired agricultural techniques ([[:en:Agriculture|Agriculture]]) while traveling through various provinces. In [[:en:1833|1833]] (Tenpō 4), he heard Shinto teachings from an elderly woman residing in [[:en:Shitaya|Shimotani]] and {{ill|Ikenohata|ja|池之端|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenohata|1=Ikenohata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551510}}, and, following his father's will, resolved to master Shinto. In the following year, [[:en:1834|1834]] (Tenpō 5), he entered the {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}} and studied its teachings.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> In [[:en:1836|1836]] (Tenpō 7), he obtained a permit for Shinto worship (神拝式許状) from the Hakukawa family.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> In [[:en:1838|1838]] (Tenpō 9), he received a priestly permit (神職許状) and the complete esoteric transmission of the three types of ritual purification (天津祝詞太祝詞三種祓等の奥旨皆伝) from the Hakukawa family. He had many disciples; among them was [[:en:Matsudaira Munehide|Munehide Matsudaira]], who later became the Grand Priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] and was a disciple from the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] of the [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]].
=== Preaching by the Founder === On April 15, [[:en:1840|1840]] (Tenpō 11), Inoue became a Shinto priest at {{ill|Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|ja|梅田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|1=Umedashinmeigu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538638}} in [[:en:武州|Bushū]], {{ill|Adachi District|ja|足立郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi District|1=Adachi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637033}}, and began his preaching, which Misogikyo regards as the establishment of its faith. However, in November [[:en:1841|1841]] (Tenpō 12),<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> he, along with his wife {{ill|Otoko Anzai|ja|安西男也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko Anzai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his disciple the Miura couple, {{ill|Hayato Miura|ja|三浦隼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chizen Miura|ja|智善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizen Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was imprisoned by the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Temples and Shrines Magistrate]].<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> Although Hayato Miura died in prison, in February [[:en:1842|1842]] (Tenpō 13) Inoue and his companions were released. He was ordered to document the teachings he had imparted and submit them to the magistrate. Inoue authored the "Shinto: The Only Q&A" (two volumes, which later became the first canonical text of Misogikyo) and submitted it; however, the suspicions were not dispelled, and in November of the same year he was imprisoned again. In June [[:en:1843|1843]] (Tenpō 14), he was exiled to [[:en:Miyake-jima|Miyakejima]] as a penal exile ([[:en:Penal transportation|Exile]]). Disciples including Munehide Matsudaira sent supplies to support him, and Inoue sent letters from the island—which were later compiled as the "Legacy Collection" in seven volumes, becoming Misogikyo's second canonical text—to instruct his followers. He continued his preaching and medical activities on Miyakejima, but died there in [[:en:1849|1849]] (Kaei 2).<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref>
== Deities == === Religious Corporation Misogikyo & Misogi Shrine === * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōkami|ja|天照太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} (Amaterasu Ōkami) * Tennotoku Chitoku Nari‑mi‑sosogi Jizai‑shin (天徳地徳乍身曽岐自在神), the deity enshrining the founder, {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}

(Takamimusubi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} (Kamimusubi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami) *** {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾神|lt=Izanagi|lt_ja=伊那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Haraito Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraito Ōkami|1=Haraedo no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588504}} (Haraito‑no‑Ōkami) *** [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusuna Ōkami]] (Ubusuna‑no‑Ōkami) * Founder Shrine ({{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|井上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22117841}}) ** Founder Inoue Masakane God *** Founder {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, the Great Divine Spirit ** Ancestral Divine Spirit of the Founder *** Ancestral and Generational Divine Spirits of the Founder **** Umasu Michitama‑no‑mikoto, Ando Shintei, venerable (Inoue Masakane's father) **** Chiyodamahime‑no‑mikoto, Ide Chiyotōjiko (Inoue Masakane's mother) **** Misosogi Tsu‑hime‑no‑mikoto, Anzai Otone (Inoue Masakane's wife) ** Ancestral Head Priest Divine Spirits of the Family *** Shōdō Iwachakukone‑no‑mikoto, Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, venerable (father of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Kareido Kōseki Dainin, Founder, Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, venerable (a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Kinshidō Kōji Konome‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's first head priest, Sakata Yasuji, venerable (son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) *** Tsugihokukyūnouchi‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's second head priest, Inui Kyūsaburō, venerable *** Tsugihoshinsakihiko‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's third head priest, Shiba Mashinju, venerable *** Mizumotokohikodomori‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's fourth head priest, Sakata Minoru, venerable (son of Sakata Yasuji) ** Generational Divine Spirits of the Misogikyo Administrators *** Tokoyo Kagami Hiko‑no‑mikoto, Miura Hayato, venerable (a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Tokoyo Kagami Hime‑no‑mikoto, Miura Ayanadōjiko (wife of Miura Hayato, disciple of Inoue Masakane) * Ancestral Spirit Hall (Clan Ancestor Spirits) ** Nationwide Clan Ancestral Divine Spirits
== Successive Religious Leaders == * First Religious Leader: Sakata Tetsu‑yasu (1872 (Meiji 5), established the Weishin Church Misogikyo and served as president. Founder) * Second Religious Leader: Sakata Yasuji (1894 (Meiji 27), first head priest. Son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) * Third Religious Leader: Inui Kyūsaburō (1900 (Meiji 33), second head priest) * Fourth Religious Leader: Shiba Mashinju (1916 (Taishō 5), third head priest) * Fifth Religious Leader: Sakata Minoru (1924 (Taishō 13), fourth head priest. Son of Sakata Yasuji) * Sixth Religious Leader: Sakata Yasugi (1957 (Shōwa 32), fifth head priest. Son of Sakata Minoru) * (Seventh Religious Leader): Sakata Yasuhiro (1988 (Shōwa 63), declared the succession of the lineage. Son of Sakata Yasugi)
== Religious Influence == According to the Ministry of Culture's "Religious Yearbook" for the first year of Reiwa, the adherents of Religious Corporation Misogikyo number 90,711, and those of Religious Corporation Shinto Misogikyo number 22,360. Combined, they amount to roughly one‑third of the prewar figures. In addition, due to the internal turmoil within Misogikyo mentioned above, several independent churches that split from the sect exist.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Misogikyo]][[en:Misogikyo]][[ja:禊教]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11591200]] Wikidata name: Misogikyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on [[:en:English Wikipedia|English Wikipedia]] {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}}. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Misogikyo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} here [[:every:Misogikyo|Misogikyo]]. {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー会話:Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User talk:Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} as a Wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} page may have been under a different title {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー会話:Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User talk:Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC submission|t||ts=20230320104937|u=Immanuelle|ns=118|demo=}}
{{notability|date=March 2023}}
{{Short description|Shinto sect}} {{nihongo|'''Misogikyo'''|禊教}} is a [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen Shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref>
It is considered a "purification sect" alongside {{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|Shinshu-kyo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>
Its name literally means [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] religion
The group is quite obscure today<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Sawada |first=Janine Anderson |date=1998 |title=Mind and Morality in Nineteenth-Century Japanese Religions: Misogi-Kyō and Maruyama-Kyō |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927 |journal=Philosophy East and West |volume=48 |issue=1 |pages=108–141 |doi=10.2307/1399927 |issn=0031-8221}}</ref> It is very ritual‑focused with little theoretical theology. In this way it contrasts with [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shinto]].<ref name=":0" /> It emphasizes the right state of mind and self‑control<ref name=":0" /> It has influence from {{ill|Confucian Shinto|ja|儒教神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucian Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but is its own tradition.<ref name=":0" />
== Sources == * https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927
== Overview == {{infobox organization | name = '''Misogikyo''' (禊教) | image = File:P1000772 misogi 2.JPG | image_size = 280 | image_caption = {{ill|Misogi Shrine|ja|身曾岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi Shrine|1=Misogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637232}} | image2 = | image_size2 = | image_caption2 = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E}}}} | abbreviation = | established = 1840 (Tenpō 11) | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} | status = | purpose = | headquarters = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]]小淵沢町上笹尾3401 | coords = {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage DMS|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E|display=inline,title}} | location = | language = | founder = {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} | notable_people = | affiliated_organizations = | institutions = | leader_title = Head Priest | leader_name = {{ill|Yasugi Sakata|ja|坂田安儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi Sakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | website = }}
'''Misogikyo''' (禊教) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] that inherits the teachings of {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, who studied Zen, physiognomy, medicine, and {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}}. It is under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology]] and is a {{ill|Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja|包括宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗教法人|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}}.
== Founder == {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} (Inoue Masakane, Founder). He was born on August 4, [[:en:1790|1790]] (Kansei 2) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]], {{ill|Nihonbashi Hamacho|ja|日本橋浜町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Hamacho|1=Nihonbashi-Hamachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508340}}, as the son of [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]] accountant and Ando Shintei (安東真鐵). He was adopted by a relative on his mother's side and took the Inoue surname. His father, Ando Shintei (父安藤真鐵), was a disciple of [[:en:Kamo no Mabuchi|Kamo Mafuchi]] and had studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]], {{ill|Medicine|ja|医術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medicine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 医療|1=health care|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31207}}, [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]], and [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]]. Although he also mastered the esoteric secrets of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], being already of advanced age, he was unable to pass them on and is said to have bequeathed to Masakane Inoue the task of mastering and spreading Shinto to the world.
== History == === Founder’s Training Period === During his youth, Masakane Inoue studied [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] and medicine under {{ill|Isono Kōdō|ja|磯野弘道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Kōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:1809|1809]] (Bunka 6), and from [[:en:1814|1814]] (Bunka 11) he learned physiognomy and methods of breath regulation from physiognomist {{ill|Mizuno Nanboku|ja|水野南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Nanboku|1=Nanboku Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549357}}. It is said that he also acquired agricultural techniques ([[:en:Agriculture|Agriculture]]) while traveling through various provinces. In [[:en:1833|1833]] (Tenpō 4), he heard Shinto teachings from an elderly woman residing in [[:en:Shitaya|Shimotani]] and {{ill|Ikenohata|ja|池之端|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenohata|1=Ikenohata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551510}}, and, following his father's will, resolved to master Shinto. In the following year, [[:en:1834|1834]] (Tenpō 5), he entered the {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}} and studied its teachings.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> In [[:en:1836|1836]] (Tenpō 7), he obtained a permit for Shinto worship (神拝式許状) from the Hakukawa family.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> In [[:en:1838|1838]] (Tenpō 9), he received a priestly permit (神職許状) and the complete esoteric transmission of the three types of ritual purification (天津祝詞太祝詞三種祓等の奥旨皆伝) from the Hakukawa family. He had many disciples; among them was [[:en:Matsudaira Munehide|Munehide Matsudaira]], who later became the Grand Priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] and was a disciple from the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] of the [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]].
=== Preaching by the Founder === On April 15, [[:en:1840|1840]] (Tenpō 11), Inoue became a Shinto priest at {{ill|Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|ja|梅田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|1=Umedashinmeigu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538638}} in [[:en:武州|Bushū]], {{ill|Adachi District|ja|足立郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi District|1=Adachi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637033}}, and began his preaching, which Misogikyo regards as the establishment of its faith. However, in November [[:en:1841|1841]] (Tenpō 12),<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> he, along with his wife {{ill|Otoko Anzai|ja|安西男也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko Anzai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his disciple the Miura couple, {{ill|Hayato Miura|ja|三浦隼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chizen Miura|ja|智善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizen Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was imprisoned by the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Temples and Shrines Magistrate]].<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> Although Hayato Miura died in prison, in February [[:en:1842|1842]] (Tenpō 13) Inoue and his companions were released. He was ordered to document the teachings he had imparted and submit them to the magistrate. Inoue authored the "Shinto: The Only Q&A" (two volumes, which later became the first canonical text of Misogikyo) and submitted it; however, the suspicions were not dispelled, and in November of the same year he was imprisoned again. In June [[:en:1843|1843]] (Tenpō 14), he was exiled to [[:en:Miyake-jima|Miyakejima]] as a penal exile ([[:en:Penal transportation|Exile]]). Disciples including Munehide Matsudaira sent supplies to support him, and Inoue sent letters from the island—which were later compiled as the "Legacy Collection" in seven volumes, becoming Misogikyo's second canonical text—to instruct his followers. He continued his preaching and medical activities on Miyakejima, but died there in [[:en:1849|1849]] (Kaei 2).<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref>
== Deities == === Religious Corporation Misogikyo & Misogi Shrine === * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōkami|ja|天照太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} (Amaterasu Ōkami) * Tennotoku Chitoku Nari‑mi‑sosogi Jizai‑shin (天徳地徳乍身曽岐自在神), the deity enshrining the founder, {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}
=== Religious Corporation Shinto Misogikyo === * Main Sanctuary (Principal Deity) ** Misogikyo Principal Deity (Misogi Oshie no Ōkami, one of the nine pillars of {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}}) *** [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (Ame‑no‑Minakanushi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} (Takamimusubi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} (Kamimusubi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami) *** {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾神|lt=Izanagi|lt_ja=伊那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo‑no‑Kami) *** {{ill|Haraito Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraito Ōkami|1=Haraedo no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588504}} (Haraito‑no‑Ōkami) *** [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusuna Ōkami]] (Ubusuna‑no‑Ōkami) * Founder Shrine ({{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|井上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22117841}}) ** Founder Inoue Masakane God *** Founder {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, the Great Divine Spirit ** Ancestral Divine Spirit of the Founder *** Ancestral and Generational Divine Spirits of the Founder **** Umasu Michitama‑no‑mikoto, Ando Shintei, venerable (Inoue Masakane's father) **** Chiyodamahime‑no‑mikoto, Ide Chiyotōjiko (Inoue Masakane's mother) **** Misosogi Tsu‑hime‑no‑mikoto, Anzai Otone (Inoue Masakane's wife) ** Ancestral Head Priest Divine Spirits of the Family *** Shōdō Iwachakukone‑no‑mikoto, Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, venerable (father of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Kareido Kōseki Dainin, Founder, Sakata Tetsu‑yasu, venerable (a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Kinshidō Kōji Konome‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's first head priest, Sakata Yasuji, venerable (son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) *** Tsugihokukyūnouchi‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's second head priest, Inui Kyūsaburō, venerable *** Tsugihoshinsakihiko‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's third head priest, Shiba Mashinju, venerable *** Mizumotokohikodomori‑no‑mikoto, Misogikyo's fourth head priest, Sakata Minoru, venerable (son of Sakata Yasuji) ** Generational Divine Spirits of the Misogikyo Administrators *** Tokoyo Kagami Hiko‑no‑mikoto, Miura Hayato, venerable (a disciple of Inoue Masakane) *** Tokoyo Kagami Hime‑no‑mikoto, Miura Ayanadōjiko (wife of Miura Hayato, disciple of Inoue Masakane) * Ancestral Spirit Hall (Clan Ancestor Spirits) ** Nationwide Clan Ancestral Divine Spirits
== Successive Religious Leaders == * First Religious Leader: Sakata Tetsu‑yasu (1872 (Meiji 5), established the Weishin Church Misogikyo and served as president. Founder) * Second Religious Leader: Sakata Yasuji (1894 (Meiji 27), first head priest. Son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) * Third Religious Leader: Inui Kyūsaburō (1900 (Meiji 33), second head priest) * Fourth Religious Leader: Shiba Mashinju (1916 (Taishō 5), third head priest) * Fifth Religious Leader: Sakata Minoru (1924 (Taishō 13), fourth head priest. Son of Sakata Yasuji) * Sixth Religious Leader: Sakata Yasugi (1957 (Shōwa 32), fifth head priest. Son of Sakata Minoru) * (Seventh Religious Leader): Sakata Yasuhiro (1988 (Shōwa 63), declared the succession of the lineage. Son of Sakata Yasugi)
== Religious Influence == According to the Ministry of Culture's "Religious Yearbook" for the first year of Reiwa, the adherents of Religious Corporation Misogikyo number 90,711, and those of Religious Corporation Shinto Misogikyo number 22,360. Combined, they amount to roughly one‑third of the prewar figures. In addition, due to the internal turmoil within Misogikyo mentioned above, several independent churches that split from the sect exist.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Misogikyo]][[en:Misogikyo]][[ja:禊教]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11591200]] Wikidata name: Misogikyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on [[:en:English Wikipedia|English Wikipedia]] {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}}. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Misogikyo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} here [[:every:Misogikyo|Misogikyo]]. {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー会話:Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User talk:Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} as a Wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the {{ill|EverybodyWiki|ja|エブリバディウィキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EverybodyWiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490864}} page may have been under a different title {{ill|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー会話:Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User talk:Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC submission|t||ts=20230320104937|u=Immanuelle|ns=118|demo=}}
{{notability|date=March 2023}}
{{Short description|Shinto sect}} {{nihongo|'''Misogikyo'''|禊教}} is a [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen Shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref>
It is considered a "purification sect" alongside {{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|Shinshu-kyo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>
Its name literally means [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] religion
The group is quite obscure today<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Sawada |first=Janine Anderson |date=1998 |title=Mind and Morality in Nineteenth-Century Japanese Religions: Misogi-Kyō and Maruyama-Kyō |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927 |journal=Philosophy East and West |volume=48 |issue=1 |pages=108–141 |doi=10.2307/1399927 |issn=0031-8221}}</ref> It is very ritual‑focused with little theoretical theology. In this way it contrasts with [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shinto]].<ref name=":0" /> It emphasizes the right state of mind and self‑control<ref name=":0" /> It has influence from {{ill|Confucian Shinto|ja|儒教神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucian Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but is its own tradition.<ref name=":0" />
== Sources == * https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927
== Overview == {{infobox organization | name = '''Misogikyo''' (禊教) | image = File:P1000772 misogi 2.JPG | image_size = 280 | image_caption = {{ill|Misogi Shrine|ja|身曾岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi Shrine|1=Misogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637232}} | image2 = | image_size2 = | image_caption2 = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E}}}} | abbreviation = | established = 1840 (Tenpō 11) | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} | status = | purpose = | headquarters = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]]小淵沢町上笹尾3401 | coords = {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage DMS|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E|display=inline,title}} | location = | language = | founder = {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} | notable_people = | affiliated_organizations = | institutions = | leader_title = Head Priest | leader_name = {{ill|Yasugi Sakata|ja|坂田安儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi Sakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | website = }}
'''Misogikyo''' (禊教) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] that inherits the teachings of {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, who studied Zen, physiognomy, medicine, and {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}}. It is under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology]] and is a {{ill|Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja|包括宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗教法人|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}}.
== Founder == {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} (Inoue Masakane, Founder). He was born on August 4, [[:en:1790|1790]] (Kansei 2) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]], {{ill|Nihonbashi Hamacho|ja|日本橋浜町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Hamacho|1=Nihonbashi-Hamachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508340}}, as the son of [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]] accountant and Ando Shintei (安東真鐵). He was adopted by a relative on his mother's side and took the Inoue surname. His father, Ando Shintei (父安藤真鐵), was a disciple of [[:en:Kamo no Mabuchi|Kamo Mafuchi]] and had studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]], {{ill|Medicine|ja|医術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medicine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 医療|1=health care|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31207}}, [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]], and [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]]. Although he also mastered the esoteric secrets of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], being already of advanced age, he was unable to pass them on and is said to have bequeathed to Masakane Inoue the task of mastering and spreading Shinto to the world.
== History == === Founder’s Training Period === During his youth, Masakane Inoue studied [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] and medicine under {{ill|Isono Kōdō|ja|磯野弘道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Kōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:1809|1809]] (Bunka 6), and from [[:en:1814|1814]] (Bunka 11) he learned physiognomy and methods of breath regulation from physiognomist {{ill|Mizuno Nanboku|ja|水野南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Nanboku|1=Nanboku Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549357}}. It is said that he also acquired agricultural techniques ([[:en:Agriculture|Agriculture]]) while traveling through various provinces. In [[:en:1833|1833]] (Tenpō 4), he heard Shinto teachings from an elderly woman residing in [[:en:Shitaya|Shimotani]] and {{ill|Ikenohata|ja|池之端|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenohata|1=Ikenohata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551510}}, and, following his father's will, resolved to master Shinto. In the following year, [[:en:1834|1834]] (Tenpō 5), he entered the {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}} and studied its teachings.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> In [[:en:1836|1836]] (Tenpō 7), he obtained a permit for Shinto worship (神拝式許状) from the Hakukawa family.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> In [[:en:1838|1838]] (Tenpō 9), he received a priestly permit (神職許状) and the complete esoteric transmission of the three types of ritual purification (天津祝詞太祝詞三種祓等の奥旨皆伝) from the Hakukawa family. He had many disciples; among them was [[:en:Matsudaira Munehide|Munehide Matsudaira]], who later became the Grand Priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] and was a disciple from the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] of the [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]].
=== Preaching by the Founder === On April 15, [[:en:1840|1840]] (Tenpō 11), Inoue became a Shinto priest at {{ill|Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|ja|梅田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|1=Umedashinmeigu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538638}} in [[:en:武州|Bushū]], {{ill|Adachi District|ja|足立郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi District|1=Adachi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637033}}, and began his preaching, which Misogikyo regards as the establishment of its faith. However, in November [[:en:1841|1841]] (Tenpō 12),<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> he, along with his wife {{ill|Otoko Anzai|ja|安西男也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko Anzai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his disciple the Miura couple, {{ill|Hayato Miura|ja|三浦隼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chizen Miura|ja|智善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizen Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was imprisoned by the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Temples and Shrines Magistrate]].<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> Although Hayato Miura died in prison, in February [[:en:1842|1842]] (Tenpō 13) Inoue and his companions were released. He was ordered to document the teachings he had imparted and submit them to the magistrate. Inoue authored the "Shinto: The Only Q&A" (two volumes, which later became the first canonical text of Misogikyo) and submitted it; however, the suspicions were not dispelled, and in November of the same year he was imprisoned again. In June [[:en:1843|1843]] (Tenpō 14), he was exiled to [[:en:Miyake-jima|Miyakejima]] as a penal exile ([[:en:Penal transportation|Exile]]). Disciples including Munehide Matsudaira sent supplies to support him, and Inoue sent letters from the island—which were later compiled as the "Legacy Collection" in seven volumes, becoming Misogikyo's second canonical text—to instruct his followers. He continued his preaching and medical activities on Miyakejima, but died there in [[:en:1849|1849]] (Kaei 2).<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref>

== Successive Religious Leaders == * First Religious Leader: Sakata Tetsu‑yasu (1872 (Meiji 5), established the Weishin Church Misogikyo and served as president. Founder) * Second Religious Leader: Sakata Yasuji (1894 (Meiji 27), first head priest. Son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) * Third Religious Leader: Inui Kyūsaburō (1900 (Meiji 33), second head priest) * Fourth Religious Leader: Shiba Mashinju (1916 (Taishō 5), third head priest) * Fifth Religious Leader: Sakata Minoru (1924 (Taishō 13), fourth head priest. Son of Sakata Yasuji) * Sixth Religious Leader: Sakata Yasugi (1957 (Shōwa 32), fifth head priest. Son of Sakata Minoru) * (Seventh Religious Leader): Sakata Yasuhiro (1988 (Shōwa 63), declared the succession of the lineage. Son of Sakata Yasugi)
== Religious Influence == According to the Ministry of Culture's "Religious Yearbook" for the first year of Reiwa, the adherents of Religious Corporation Misogikyo number 90,711, and those of Religious Corporation Shinto Misogikyo number 22,360. Combined, they amount to roughly one‑third of the prewar figures. In addition, due to the internal turmoil within Misogikyo mentioned above, several independent churches that split from the sect exist.

{{Short description|Shinto sect}} {{nihongo|'''Misogikyo'''|禊教}} is a [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen Shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref>
It is considered a "purification sect" alongside {{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|Shinshu-kyo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>
Its name literally means [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]] religion
The group is quite obscure today<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Sawada |first=Janine Anderson |date=1998 |title=Mind and Morality in Nineteenth-Century Japanese Religions: Misogi-Kyō and Maruyama-Kyō |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927 |journal=Philosophy East and West |volume=48 |issue=1 |pages=108–141 |doi=10.2307/1399927 |issn=0031-8221}}</ref> It is very ritual‑focused with little theoretical theology. In this way it contrasts with [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shinto]].<ref name=":0" /> It emphasizes the right state of mind and self‑control<ref name=":0" /> It has influence from {{ill|Confucian Shinto|ja|儒教神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Confucian Shinto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but is its own tradition.<ref name=":0" />
== Sources == * https://www.jstor.org/stable/1399927
== Overview == {{infobox organization | name = '''Misogikyo''' (禊教) | image = File:P1000772 misogi 2.JPG | image_size = 280 | image_caption = {{ill|Misogi Shrine|ja|身曾岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misogi Shrine|1=Misogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637232}} | image2 = | image_size2 = | image_caption2 = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E}}}} | abbreviation = | established = 1840 (Tenpō 11) | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} | status = | purpose = | headquarters = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City]]小淵沢町上笹尾3401 | coords = {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage DMS|35|51|46.1|N|138|20|12.5|E|display=inline,title}} | location = | language = | founder = {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} | notable_people = | affiliated_organizations = | institutions = | leader_title = Head Priest | leader_name = {{ill|Yasugi Sakata|ja|坂田安儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi Sakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | website = }}
'''Misogikyo''' (禊教) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shinto]] that inherits the teachings of {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}}, who studied Zen, physiognomy, medicine, and {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}}. It is under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology]] and is a {{ill|Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja|包括宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Religious Corporation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗教法人|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}}.
== Founder == {{ill|Masakane Inoue|ja|井上正鐵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masakane Inoue|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 井上正鉄|1=Masakane Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535816}} (Inoue Masakane, Founder). He was born on August 4, [[:en:1790|1790]] (Kansei 2) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]], {{ill|Nihonbashi Hamacho|ja|日本橋浜町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Hamacho|1=Nihonbashi-Hamachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508340}}, as the son of [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]] accountant and Ando Shintei (安東真鐵). He was adopted by a relative on his mother's side and took the Inoue surname. His father, Ando Shintei (父安藤真鐵), was a disciple of [[:en:Kamo no Mabuchi|Kamo Mafuchi]] and had studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]], {{ill|Medicine|ja|医術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medicine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 医療|1=health care|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31207}}, [[:en:Confucianism|Confucianism]], and [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]]. Although he also mastered the esoteric secrets of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]], being already of advanced age, he was unable to pass them on and is said to have bequeathed to Masakane Inoue the task of mastering and spreading Shinto to the world.
== History == === Founder’s Training Period === During his youth, Masakane Inoue studied [[:en:Zen|Zen Buddhism]] and medicine under {{ill|Isono Kōdō|ja|磯野弘道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Kōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:1809|1809]] (Bunka 6), and from [[:en:1814|1814]] (Bunka 11) he learned physiognomy and methods of breath regulation from physiognomist {{ill|Mizuno Nanboku|ja|水野南北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Nanboku|1=Nanboku Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549357}}. It is said that he also acquired agricultural techniques ([[:en:Agriculture|Agriculture]]) while traveling through various provinces. In [[:en:1833|1833]] (Tenpō 4), he heard Shinto teachings from an elderly woman residing in [[:en:Shitaya|Shimotani]] and {{ill|Ikenohata|ja|池之端|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenohata|1=Ikenohata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551510}}, and, following his father's will, resolved to master Shinto. In the following year, [[:en:1834|1834]] (Tenpō 5), he entered the {{ill|Hakuji Shinto|ja|伯家神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuji Shinto|1=Hakke Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381473}} and studied its teachings.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> In [[:en:1836|1836]] (Tenpō 7), he obtained a permit for Shinto worship (神拝式許状) from the Hakukawa family.<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> In [[:en:1838|1838]] (Tenpō 9), he received a priestly permit (神職許状) and the complete esoteric transmission of the three types of ritual purification (天津祝詞太祝詞三種祓等の奥旨皆伝) from the Hakukawa family. He had many disciples; among them was [[:en:Matsudaira Munehide|Munehide Matsudaira]], who later became the Grand Priest of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] and was a disciple from the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] of the [[:en:Miyazu Domain|Miyazu Domain]].
=== Preaching by the Founder === On April 15, [[:en:1840|1840]] (Tenpō 11), Inoue became a Shinto priest at {{ill|Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|ja|梅田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Shinmyō Shrine|1=Umedashinmeigu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538638}} in [[:en:武州|Bushū]], {{ill|Adachi District|ja|足立郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi District|1=Adachi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637033}}, and began his preaching, which Misogikyo regards as the establishment of its faith. However, in November [[:en:1841|1841]] (Tenpō 12),<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref> he, along with his wife {{ill|Otoko Anzai|ja|安西男也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko Anzai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his disciple the Miura couple, {{ill|Hayato Miura|ja|三浦隼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chizen Miura|ja|智善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizen Miura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was imprisoned by the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Temples and Shrines Magistrate]].<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō|1991|p=16}}</ref> Although Hayato Miura died in prison, in February [[:en:1842|1842]] (Tenpō 13) Inoue and his companions were released. He was ordered to document the teachings he had imparted and submit them to the magistrate. Inoue authored the "Shinto: The Only Q&A" (two volumes, which later became the first canonical text of Misogikyo) and submitted it; however, the suspicions were not dispelled, and in November of the same year he was imprisoned again. In June [[:en:1843|1843]] (Tenpō 14), he was exiled to [[:en:Miyake-jima|Miyakejima]] as a penal exile ([[:en:Penal transportation|Exile]]). Disciples including Munehide Matsudaira sent supplies to support him, and Inoue sent letters from the island—which were later compiled as the "Legacy Collection" in seven volumes, becoming Misogikyo's second canonical text—to instruct his followers. He continued his preaching and medical activities on Miyakejima, but died there in [[:en:1849|1849]] (Kaei 2).<ref>{{sfn|Inoue Junkō et al.|1996|pp=359-360}}</ref>

== Successive Religious Leaders == * First Religious Leader: Sakata Tetsu‑yasu (1872 (Meiji 5), established the Weishin Church Misogikyo and served as president. Founder) * Second Religious Leader: Sakata Yasuji (1894 (Meiji 27), first head priest. Son of Sakata Tetsu‑yasu) * Third Religious Leader: Inui Kyūsaburō (1900 (Meiji 33), second head priest) * Fourth Religious Leader: Shiba Mashinju (1916 (Taishō 5), third head priest) * Fifth Religious Leader: Sakata Minoru (1924 (Taishō 13), fourth head priest. Son of Sakata Yasuji) * Sixth Religious Leader: Sakata Yasugi (1957 (Shōwa 32), fifth head priest. Son of Sakata Minoru) * (Seventh Religious Leader): Sakata Yasuhiro (1988 (Shōwa 63), declared the succession of the lineage. Son of Sakata Yasugi)
== Religious Influence == According to the Ministry of Culture's "Religious Yearbook" for the first year of Reiwa, the adherents of Religious Corporation Misogikyo number 90,711, and those of Religious Corporation Shinto Misogikyo number 22,360. Combined, they amount to roughly one‑third of the prewar figures. In addition, due to the internal turmoil within Misogikyo mentioned above, several independent churches that split from the sect exist.
